meaning: n.舞台造型,(由活人扮演的)静态画面、场面
examples: Some composed careful tableaux of empty clothing and abandoned dinners.
He developed less a coherent analysis than a series of brilliant tableaux.
The projects in the video are just a tiny corner of an incredible tableaux of development happening globally!
But the sculptor isn't about to turn out tableaux in stone or wood anytime soon.
Calendars showing tasteful tableaux of jam-making housewives, women footballers or soldiers' girlfriends have been a fine way to raise funds for good causes in Britain.
